steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The clause after the blank states that years pass between a soil's selection and its official designation, showing that this time gap happens often.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification/frequency — the blank must signal that the time gap described (years passing) is a common occurrence, not an exception.
  • Choice A — "In many cases,": signals frequency, fitting the claim that years often pass between selection and designation. ✓
  • Choice B — "Similarly,": signals similarity to a preceding idea, but no parallel idea precedes the blank — the passage gives examples then makes a general claim. ✗
  • Choice C — "Indeed,": signals emphasis or confirmation, not frequency; it would work if restating something already established, but the time-gap claim is new information. ✗
  • Choice D — "Therefore,": signals a conclusion caused by prior facts, but the sentence doesn't follow logically from the Oklahoma/Maine examples — it introduces a new general pattern. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
